title: Downloading VM image console lines are censored to chars followed by ellipsis
body:
### Issue Description

Maybe someone can explain the reasoning behind what looks like an anti-feature logging feature where podman cuts every character after 60th and appends ellipsis ('...'):

```
Downloading VM image: fedora-coreos-37.20230303.2.0-qemu.x8…
Downloading VM image: fedora-coreos-37.20230303.2.0-qemu.x8…
Downloading VM image: fedora-coreos-37.20230303.2.0-qemu.x8…
Downloading VM image: fedora-coreos-37.20230303.2.0-qemu.x8…
Downloading VM image: fedora-coreos-37.20230303.2.0-qemu.x8…
```


### Steps to reproduce the issue

See https://github.com/ansible/vscode-ansible/actions/runs/4366041564/jobs/7635510663#step:9:34

### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

### Describe the results you expected

No censoring

### podman info output

```yaml
podman 4.4.2
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I have same problem.

I need detect progress in my nodejs app.

Podman set default Width=60
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/060c125b783f1f6202e1646426f718469cb2720d/pkg/machine/pull.go#L170

mbp detect terminal width here
https://github.com/vbauerster/mpb/blob/60781358ad4ecc6acbc48a7a709e482d25e2ce19/cwriter/writer_posix.go#L37

When i spawned command **podman machine init**, mbp cannot detect width, because nodejs not set tty for detect width
@viplifes I made a change to increase it to 80 but I am afraid that we might need a bigger default value like 100 to make it practical.

I think 80 characters might not be enough.

String -
"Downloading VM image: fedora-coreos-37.20230401.2.0-qemu.x86_64.qcow2.xz [---] 5.6MiB / 649.3MiB"
already has 96 characters.

Maybe it makes no sense to limit the width so much? Maybe we can set 150?
